definition: a departure from what is normal, usual, or expected
example: The unusually cold summer was a temporary aberration, not a lasting trend.

definition: to leave hurriedly and secretly, typically to avoid capture or the law
example: The treasurer absconded with the club's funds before anyone noticed.

definition: to voluntarily refrain from doing something
example: She chose to abstain from voting because she felt uninformed on the issue.

definition: to warn or reprimand someone firmly but not harshly
example: The teacher admonished the students for talking during the exam.

definition: concerned with beauty or the appreciation of beauty
example: The building was praised for its clean, minimalist aesthetic appeal.

definition: to increase the power, status, or wealth of; to exaggerate
example: The ruler built lavish monuments to aggrandize his own reputation.

definition: brisk and cheerful readiness or eagerness
example: She accepted the challenge with surprising alacrity.

definition: to combine or unite to form one organization or structure
example: The two small firms amalgamated into a single large corporation.

definition: having mixed or contradictory feelings about something
example: He felt ambivalent about the promotion, excited yet anxious.

definition: to make a bad or unpleasant situation better
example: New policies were introduced to ameliorate the housing shortage.

definition: something placed in the wrong historical period
example: A smartphone in a medieval film would be a glaring anachronism.

definition: a deep-seated feeling of dislike or aversion
example: There was clear antipathy between the two rival candidates.

definition: lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern
example: Voter apathy led to a disappointingly low turnout.

definition: understood by few; mysterious or secret
example: The manual was full of arcane terminology only experts could follow.

definition: to make an unpleasant feeling less intense; to soothe
example: A sincere apology did much to assuage her anger.

definition: showing a willingness to take bold risks; daring
example: The general devised an audacious plan to cross the mountains at night.

definition: severe or strict in manner; plain and without comfort
example: The monks led an austere life free of luxury.

definition: to give a false impression of; to contradict
example: Her calm expression belied the panic she felt inside.

definition: well meaning and kindly
example: The benevolent donor funded scholarships for hundreds of students.

definition: to support or strengthen
example: New evidence helped bolster the prosecution's case.

definition: a harsh, discordant mixture of sounds
example: The cacophony of car horns filled the crowded street.

definition: given to sudden and unpredictable changes in mood or behavior
example: The capricious weather ruined our carefully planned picnic.

definition: to reprimand or criticize someone severely
example: The critic castigated the author for careless research.

definition: sarcastic and biting; capable of burning by chemical action
example: His caustic remarks left the interns feeling humiliated.

definition: cautious and careful to consider all circumstances and consequences
example: She was circumspect about revealing details of the negotiation.

definition: clear, logical, and convincing
example: The lawyer presented a cogent argument that swayed the jury.

definition: an expert judge in matters of taste, especially the fine arts
example: A true connoisseur of wine can identify the region from a single sip.

definition: a confusing and difficult problem or question
example: How to fund the project without raising taxes was a real conundrum.

definition: to confirm or support with evidence
example: Two witnesses corroborated the driver's account of the accident.

definition: too ready to believe things; easily deceived
example: The scam preyed on credulous investors hoping for quick riches.

definition: hasty and therefore not thorough or detailed
example: A cursory glance at the report missed several serious errors.

definition: to expose the falseness of a belief or claim
example: The article set out to debunk several popular health myths.

definition: humble submission and respect toward another
example: Out of deference to his mentor, he withheld his objection.

definition: causing harm or damage
example: Excessive screen time can have a deleterious effect on sleep.

definition: to criticize unfairly; to belittle
example: It is unkind to denigrate the efforts of well-meaning volunteers.

definition: a forceful and bitter verbal attack against someone or something
example: His speech turned into a lengthy diatribe against the media.

definition: intended to teach, often with a moral lesson
example: The novel was too didactic, lecturing readers at every turn.

definition: modest or shy because of a lack of self-confidence
example: The diffident student rarely spoke up in seminars.

definition: slow to act; intended to cause delay
example: The lawyers used dilatory tactics to postpone the trial.

definition: essentially different in kind; not comparable
example: The report tried to reconcile two disparate sets of data.

definition: asserting opinions as if they were absolute truths
example: His dogmatic refusal to consider new ideas frustrated the committee.

definition: cheerful and full of energy
example: She gave an ebullient speech that lifted everyone's spirits.

definition: deriving ideas or style from a broad and diverse range of sources
example: The cafe had an eclectic decor mixing antiques and modern art.

definition: the ability to produce a desired or intended result
example: Clinical trials confirmed the efficacy of the new vaccine.

definition: to make something clear; to explain
example: The professor elucidated the theory with a simple diagram.

definition: to cause someone to feel drained of energy
example: The oppressive heat enervated the hikers within an hour.

definition: lasting for a very short time
example: Fame in the digital age can be surprisingly ephemeral.

definition: to use ambiguous language to conceal the truth or avoid commitment
example: The official equivocated when asked about the budget cuts.

definition: having or showing great knowledge or learning
example: Her erudite lecture drew on history, art, and philosophy.

definition: intended for or understood by only a small, specialized group
example: The paper explored an esoteric branch of pure mathematics.

definition: to make a problem or bad situation worse
example: Ignoring the leak only exacerbated the water damage.

definition: to praise enthusiastically
example: Critics extolled the film as a masterpiece of storytelling.

definition: very attentive to accuracy and detail; hard to please
example: The fastidious editor caught even the smallest typos.

definition: having or showing intense and passionate feeling
example: She was a fervent supporter of the environmental movement.

definition: happening by chance, often in a fortunate way
example: A fortuitous meeting at the conference led to a lasting partnership.

definition: excessively talkative, especially about trivial matters
example: The garrulous passenger chatted for the entire flight.

definition: fond of company; sociable
example: Her gregarious nature made her the center of every gathering.

definition: lacking originality through overuse; clichéd
example: The speech relied on hackneyed phrases and tired metaphors.

definition: a lengthy and aggressive speech
example: The coach delivered a furious harangue after the loss.

definition: a person who attacks cherished beliefs or institutions
example: As an iconoclast, she challenged the field's oldest assumptions.

definition: having little or no money
example: The impecunious artist could barely afford paint and canvas.

definition: acting quickly without thought or care
example: His impetuous decision to quit left him without income.

definition: just begun and so not fully formed or developed
example: The plan was still inchoate, little more than a rough sketch.

definition: wanting to avoid activity or exertion; lazy
example: The indolent employee left most of his tasks unfinished.

definition: too great or extreme to be expressed in words
example: The view from the summit filled them with ineffable joy.

definition: innocent, honest, and unsuspecting
example: Her ingenuous questions revealed how new she was to the field.

definition: tending to obstruct or harm; hostile
example: The harsh climate was inimical to sustained farming.

definition: lacking flavor, vigor, or interest; dull
example: The critics dismissed the play as insipid and forgettable.

definition: unwilling to change one's views or agree; stubborn
example: Negotiations stalled because both sides remained intransigent.

definition: having a long-established and unlikely-to-change habit
example: He was an inveterate gambler who could not resist a bet.

definition: using very few words; concise to the point of seeming rude
example: Her laconic reply of 'fine' ended the conversation.

definition: displaying a pleasant lack of energy; slow and relaxed
example: They spent a languid afternoon dozing by the pool.

definition: tending to talk a great deal; very talkative
example: The loquacious host barely let his guests get a word in.

definition: expressed clearly and easy to understand; rational
example: Despite his fever, he gave a remarkably lucid account of events.

definition: generous or forgiving, especially toward a rival
example: In victory she was magnanimous, praising her defeated opponent.

definition: not telling the truth; lying
example: The mendacious salesman misled customers about the warranty.

definition: subject to sudden or unpredictable changes of mood
example: His mercurial temper made him difficult to work with.

definition: showing great attention to detail; very careful
example: The scientist kept meticulous records of every experiment.

definition: to make less severe, serious, or painful
example: Planting trees can help mitigate the effects of flooding.

definition: sullen and ill-tempered; gloomy
example: He grew morose after hearing the disappointing news.

definition: just coming into existence and beginning to develop
example: The nascent industry attracted eager young entrepreneurs.

definition: wicked or criminal
example: The villain hatched a nefarious scheme to seize the throne.

definition: to make something obscure, unclear, or unintelligible
example: The report used jargon to obfuscate its lack of real findings.

definition: excessively obedient or attentive in a servile way
example: The obsequious waiter hovered anxiously beside every table.

definition: stubbornly refusing to change one's opinion or course
example: His obstinate refusal to compromise doomed the talks.

definition: designed to impress or attract notice; showy
example: The mansion's ostentatious decor struck guests as excessive.

definition: a person or thing regarded as a perfect example of a quality
example: She was held up as a paragon of professional integrity.

definition: unwilling to spend money or use resources; stingy
example: His parsimonious habits meant he reused every scrap of paper.

definition: the presence of something in only small or insufficient quantities
example: A paucity of evidence forced the case to be dismissed.

definition: overly concerned with minor details or formal rules
example: His pedantic corrections about grammar annoyed his colleagues.

definition: a strong or habitual liking for something
example: She has a penchant for solving difficult puzzles.

definition: deceitful and untrustworthy; treacherous
example: The perfidious ally secretly negotiated with the enemy.

definition: carried out with minimum effort or reflection; routine
example: He gave the documents only a perfunctory review before signing.

definition: having a harmful effect, especially in a gradual way
example: The pernicious rumor slowly destroyed her reputation.

definition: to make someone less angry or hostile
example: The airline offered vouchers to placate the stranded passengers.

definition: spending money or resources recklessly and wastefully
example: His prodigal lifestyle soon exhausted the family fortune.

definition: commonplace and unromantic; dull
example: Behind the exotic title lay a rather prosaic story.

definition: exceedingly idealistic and impractical
example: His quixotic plan to end all poverty overlooked harsh realities.

definition: stubbornly resistant to authority or control
example: The recalcitrant employee ignored every new company rule.

definition: to clear of blame or prove to be right
example: New evidence finally vindicated the wrongly accused man.
